articles.title: Einstein's relativity rules chemical bonds in heavy elements, new research shows
articles.summary: Researchers at Brown University have provided direct experimental evidence that the textbook explanation of how triple chemical bonds work in heavy elements breaks down under the influence of Einstein's theory of relativity. The study shows that as atomic nuclei become heavier, the principles described in this theory change, leading to a blurring of the lines between sigma and pi bonds involved in heavy element bonding. This finding has significant implications for our understanding of chemical bonding in these elements.

articles.title: Adding Go to a browser code runner
articles.summary: Adding Go to a browser code runner means setting GOOS=js and compiling Go to WASM for the browser, which involves two pieces: a compiler target that produces WASM with a JavaScript syscall bridge, and wasm_exec.js, a support file that implements the Go runtime's expectations on the JS side. This is how most in-browser Go playgrounds work, but dailyprog runs user-submitted code inside an isolated-vm with no DOM, event loop, or restricted API surface, which causes the program to hang until it times out due to a maximum call stack size exceeded error. The clean path involves using GOOS=wasip1 WASI as a system interface for WASM, which defines a set of imports that a WASM module can call and provides implementations for file I/O, environment variables, clock, and random numbers.

articles.title: What Every Python Developer Should Know About the CPython ABI
articles.summary: The CPython ABI, or Common Python Object Model, is a critical component of the Python interpreter that enables seamless interaction between Python code and native libraries. Understanding how to work with the CPython ABI is essential for developers who want to optimize their applications for performance-critical tasks. The ABI provides a standardized interface for accessing memory, performing arithmetic operations, and manipulating data structures, making it a fundamental aspect of Python development. By grasping the basics of the CPython ABI, developers can write more efficient and effective code that leverages native libraries and hardware resources.

articles.title: Good Tools Are Invisible
articles.summary: A good tool should strive to make itself invisible, not just by hiding its shortcomings but also by making them seem like a fun puzzle to solve. This is particularly true when it comes to text editors like vim, which can be frustratingly limited in their ability to handle certain tasks. When you're proficient with your editor of choice and it lacks the tools you need, it becomes invisible, but treating that friction as the "fun" part can lead people to advertise it as evidence of its greatness. In contrast, a good tool should focus on making itself useful without drawing attention to its limitations.
